description:
Letter from Ḥalfon b. Netanʾel ha-Levi, in Bharuch, northwest India, probably to Maḍmūn b. Ḥasan/Yefet, in Aden. Dating: 1134 CE. The addressee is a dignitary referred to as 'rayyis,' and as Goitein surmised, it can be assumed that the recipient is Maḍmūn b. Ḥasan, the head of the Jews of Yemen and the representative of the merchants there. Some of the business was already arranged in Aden, 'in his seat' and in his presence. The writer reports that he stayed in India for a second year. He is sending a large shipment of fabrics to Aden with Barakāt b. Mūsā al-Ḥalabi. (Information from Goitein and Friedman, India Book IV.) 

The sender was previously identified on PGP as Yiṣḥaq b. Makhlūf al-Nafūsī, maybe because Goitein's attached notes contain his jottings about the Nafūsī family members. 

On verso there are largely undeciphered mercantile accounts involving shipments of silk in Arabic script and Greek/Coptic numerals. There is also an unrelated block of text containing medical prescriptions in Arabic script. IB ח18 (ו27).
description_he: ספר הודו IV,ח18. מכתב מחלפון בן נתנאל הלוי בהרוכ', צפון-מערב הודו, כנראה למד'מון בן חסן/יפת בעדן; 1134 לספירה; מדווח שנשאר בהודו שנה שניה; שולח משלוח בדים גדול
